rules.default_asset_strategy: procedural geometry and generated materials first

assets.name: M9 Office Carpet Texture
assets.scale_notes: Tile-scaled across room floors at 6 by 6 UV repetition to preserve low-contrast first-person readability.
assets.style_notes: Muted beige-green woven office carpet, clean and corporate-surreal without dark horror drift.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with AI-generated or hand-approved tileable carpet using the same path or asset ID; preserve low contrast.

assets.name: M9 Wall Subtle Variation Texture
assets.scale_notes: Applied to procedural wall boxes with restrained 3 by 2 UV repetition.
assets.style_notes: Warm institutional drywall with faint panel seams and tiny scuffs; clean but wrong, not decayed.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with a higher-quality tileable wall texture while keeping sign readability and non-dark tone.

assets.name: M9 Ceiling Tile Light Panel Texture
assets.scale_notes: Tile-scaled across ceiling boxes at 4 by 4 repetition; does not affect collision or lighting.
assets.style_notes: Pale acoustic tile and fluorescent panel motif, low contrast and non-oppressive.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with generated ceiling tile variations that remain bright and unobtrusive.

assets.name: M9 Elevator Complete Check-Out Panel Texture
assets.scale_notes: Applied to elevator metal material and a small non-collidable check-out panel beside the elevator.
assets.style_notes: Stylized brushed metal with button-like marks, calm corporate rather than tactical or horror.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with a richer check-out/elevator panel while preserving M4/M8 File Audit behavior.

assets.name: M9 Incident Report Paper Texture
assets.scale_notes: Applied to existing paper/form-stack material without changing report placement, collection radius, or HUD behavior.
assets.style_notes: Warm form paper with pale rule lines and restrained audit-stamp geometry.
assets.replacement_notes: Future replacement may combine this with per-report generated art while preserving readable IR IDs and source-defined reports.

assets.name: M9 Clarity Abstract Overlay
assets.scale_notes: Small non-collidable records-room wall plane with alpha enabled; decorative only.
assets.style_notes: Pale cyan/gold linework suggesting bureaucratic uncertainty, not supernatural darkness.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with a higher-quality Clarity motif if M10/M11 polish calls for it.

assets.name: M9 Policy Poster - Maintain Uncertainty
assets.scale_notes: Small non-collidable lobby wall poster, offset through createWallTextureSign to avoid z-fighting.
assets.style_notes: Readable corporate policy poster with dry uncertainty language; clean, bright, low-noise.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with AI-generated poster art preserving the same text hierarchy and contrast.

assets.name: M9 Policy Poster - Optional Meetings
assets.scale_notes: Small non-collidable conference-room wall poster, offset and front-face culled like M8.1 signs.
assets.style_notes: Dry meeting-policy text, corporate-surreal and funny without chase or horror language.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with a more polished generated poster while preserving The Meeting's non-enemy tone.

assets.name: M9 Policy Poster - Records Remember
assets.scale_notes: Small non-collidable records-room wall poster with readable front-facing orientation.
assets.style_notes: Internal records notice with calm version-history absurdism.
assets.replacement_notes: Replace later with richer records-room art while preserving sign readability.

assets.name: M9 Meeting Agenda Card
assets.scale_notes: Small non-collidable conference-room agenda sign; decorative and replaceable.
assets.style_notes: Readable agenda text using dry bureaucratic weirdness, not threatening horror.
assets.replacement_notes: Future generated agenda variants can replace this without changing Meeting hazard behavior.

placeholders.name: M2 Source-Defined Room Network
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js procedural meshes from TypeScript room definitions
placeholders.scale_notes: Five connected 8 by 8 meter office rooms arranged as lobby, cubicles, conference, records, and elevator placeholder.
placeholders.style_notes: Clean fluorescent corporate-surreal rooms with readable signage and calm liminal office geometry.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future milestones can expand the room data with collectible placements, extraction behavior, Clarity variants, and hazard anchors.

placeholders.name: M1 Office Shell
placeholders.scale_notes: Single 14 by 14 meter office-like room with 3.15 meter ceiling and first-person player scale.
placeholders.style_notes: Beige carpet, fluorescent office lights, concrete columns, glass panel, clean but wrong corporate tone.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Replaced in M2 by m2-room-network; keep as historical proof only.

placeholders.name: M1 Incident Report Placeholder
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js plane with DynamicTexture text
placeholders.scale_notes: Small paper-sized plane placed on the desk at readable first-person height.
placeholders.style_notes: Readable office paperwork with restrained red accent; no external asset file.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Replaced in M3 by source-defined Incident Report collectibles; keep as historical proof only.

placeholders.name: M3 Incident Report Collectibles
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js planes with DynamicTexture text and TypeScript placement data
placeholders.scale_notes: Three paper-sized reports placed on reachable office surfaces in the lobby, cubicles, and elevator room.
placeholders.style_notes: Readable office paperwork with restrained red accents and dry corporate-surreal report text.
placeholders.replacement_notes: M9 may replace DynamicTexture paperwork with generated texture sheets while preserving report IDs and source placement data.

placeholders.name: M1 Elevator Placeholder
placeholders.scale_notes: Door-sized frame and panels on the front wall.
placeholders.style_notes: Corporate metal doorway with EXIT REQUEST PENDING sign.
placeholders.replacement_notes: M4 attached extraction behavior through a separate source-driven zone. Geometry remains a placeholder until a later approved polish or asset milestone.

placeholders.name: M4 Elevator Extraction Zone
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js procedural marker and DynamicTexture status sign
placeholders.scale_notes: Small non-collidable extraction zone marker placed in the elevator room, gated by 3 Incident Reports.
placeholders.style_notes: Restrained corporate elevator status signage with dry locked/approved/filed states.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future milestones may replace the marker/sign art or add light animation while preserving source-driven extraction rules.

placeholders.name: M5 Clarity Meter
placeholders.format: runtime DOM HUD text backed by TypeScript Clarity state
placeholders.scale_notes: Starts at 100 percent and changes through small source-defined event deltas.
placeholders.style_notes: Dry corporate status copy; subtle and non-horror.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future milestones may add sign contradictions or visual response while preserving Clarity baseline and restart reset behavior.

placeholders.name: M6 Performance Review Narrator
placeholders.format: runtime DOM HUD text backed by TypeScript message pools and narrator state
placeholders.scale_notes: Text-only narrator line with frame-based cooldowns and short event history.
placeholders.style_notes: Calm, dry, bureaucratic, surreal, lightly funny, non-dark.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future milestones may add more Performance Review triggers or presentation polish without adding voice audio before approval.

placeholders.name: M7 The Meeting Hazard
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js procedural meshes with TypeScript zone, state, and update rules
placeholders.scale_notes: Conference-room hazard using human-scale chairs, a small table, and an agenda panel that rearrange into a partial office-object pressure shape while leaving escape paths.
placeholders.style_notes: Calm corporate-surreal meeting furniture and agenda signage; no creature, enemy, gore, jump scare, or dark horror presentation.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future milestones may expand Meeting variants or object layouts while preserving source-defined trigger, escape, and non-enemy behavior.

placeholders.name: M8 Complete Run Wayfinding
placeholders.format: runtime Babylon.js wall signs with DynamicTexture text
placeholders.scale_notes: Small non-collidable wayfinding signs placed in each approved room to support the first complete five-room run without heavy tutorialization.
placeholders.style_notes: Restrained office signage using File Audit and Complete Check-Out language; no external assets or M9 visual pipeline.
placeholders.replacement_notes: Future polish may replace or restyle signs while preserving readable five-room run guidance.

placeholders.name: M1 DOM HUD Placeholder
placeholders.scale_notes: Responsive full-screen overlay sized for desktop browser smoke testing.
placeholders.style_notes: Dry corporate status panel with restart button and minimal guidance.
placeholders.replacement_notes: M3 and later should expand HUD for report count, Clarity, and system feedback.
